title: Creating a UX Widget
text: Creating a UX Widget To create a UX widget for a DSP field, an entity, or a component using the UX Widget Interface, you need to perform the following steps: Define the logical web widget definition in the usys.ini file. Use the [webwidgets] section for fields, the [webentitywidgets] section for entities, and the [webcomponentwidgets] section for components. For more information, see [webwidgets] , [webentitywidgets] , or [webcomponentwidgets] . Define the physical web widget definition in the web.ini file. For more details, see web.ini . Create the widget JavaScript class and register it with the Uniface Widget Repository with name as defined in the web.ini . For more details, see UX Widget Interface . Write implementation for the relevant interface functions. Start with a simple example. For more details, see UX Widget Interface . Test your UX Widget Interface in a DSP. Define a UX widget To define a UX widget, add the logical and physical widget definitions in the .ini files: usys.ini Explain this code [webwidgets] my-logical-field-widget = customFieldWidget [webentitywidgets] my-logical-entity-widget = customEntityWidget [webcomponentwidgets] my-logical-component-widget = customComponentWidget Copy web.ini Explain this code [customFieldWidget] widget_class = MyWidgets.SimpleFieldWidget module = $LIBURL/../my-widgets/simple-field-widget.js ulayout = TAG=span;CHARACTERS=placeholder;HTMLATTRIBUTES=id=ubinding uxInterfaceVersion = 2 [customEntityWidget] occurrence_widget_class = MyWidgets.SimpleOccurrenceWidget collection_widget_class = MyWidgets.SimpleCollectionWidget module = $LIBURL/../my-widgets/simple-coll-widget.js;$LIBURL/../my-widgets/simple-occ-widget.js uxInterfaceVersion = 2 [customComponentWidget] component_widget_class = MyWidgets.SimpleComponentWidget module = $LIBURL/../my-widgets/simple-component-widget.js ulayout = TAG=div;HTMLATTRIBUTES=id=ubinding uxInterfaceVersion = 2 Copy The JavaScript files simple-field-widget.js , simple-occ-widget.js , simple-coll-widget.js , and simple-component-widget.js implement the UX Widget API for the SimpleFieldWidget , SimpleOccurrenceWidget , SimpleCollectionWidget , and SimpleComponentWidget classes respectively. To see the custom widgets in the IDE, select one of the widgets from the DSP Widget Type section drop-down list. This drop-down shows the names of the logical widgets that are defined in the usys.ini file. Data Object Context Each widget type is bound to a specific data object: Widget Type Data Object Type Binding ID Prefix Component Component instance ucpt: Collection Entity/Collection data object uent: Occurrence Occurrence data object uocc: Field Field data object ufld: Note: You must use the component binding ID in the format id="ucpt:" . At runtime, Uniface appends the instance name to the binding ID. For example, for the component UCDSP1 , if you provide an invalid binding ID such as "ucpt:Anything" in the layout, Uniface displays the following warning message: Invalid component id ucpt:Anything detected for component UCDSP1 Please update your HTML to use the correct node ID to avoid this warning. Copy Trigger Context Triggers can be defined at each level: Level Trigger Definition Location Scope Component Component Script section Entire component Collection Entity triggers (collection context) Entire entity collection Occurrence Entity triggers (occurrence context) Single occurrence Field Field triggers in DSP Editor Single field Widget API The following widget API functions are available for all UX widget classes (field, entity, and component): processLayout constructor onConnect mapTrigger dataInit dataUpdate dataCleanup blockUI unblockUI Additional widget API functions are available for field-level UX widget classes: validate showError hideError getValue Note: The sequence and behavior of these API functions are the same for field-level, entity-level, and component-level UX widgets. Component widgets follow the same API patterns as field and entity widgets, with the validate parameter set to false in mapTrigger() as validation through the widget API is only supported for field-level widgets. processLayout() Typically, the processLayout() API function of a field widget class simply returns an HTML element, possibly with some child elements, to be used at run-time by the field widget. The processLayout() API function of a collection widget class typically returns the placeholder for its occurrence widget, and the processLayout() API function of an occurrence widget class would return the placeholder elements of the entity's field widgets. The processLayout() API function of a component widget class typically returns the root HTML structure that represents the component at run time and acts as a container for its child widgets. However, it is completely up to the UX widget class developer what the processLayout() API function returns. Some field widget classes could be complex, built up of multiple child widgets, which could be either entity or field widgets. To learn about creating a UX Widget in detail, enroll into Uniface UX e-learning course: https://learn.rocketsoftware.com/course/view.php?id=315 . Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
